structural_rule: Opening scene hook. Often present-tense or immediate past, second-person or third-person named-character cold open. Preserve sentence rhythm (short, punchy) and em-dash constructions. Do not expand into explanatory prose.

Largest single slot family by variant count (5,366). First-person author reflection, multi-paragraph. Widest word-count range (0-469) -- some CANONICAL.txt REFLECTION blocks are empty stubs (0 words); do not invent content for empty source, report as blocker per translate-zh-tw.md rule.

The 7 engine dirs (comparison, false_alarm, grief, overwhelm, shame, spiral, watcher) are near-identical in structural shape (word count, sentence count) -- they are the emotional/mechanism core of each persona x topic cell. Preserve paragraph breaks; each engine dir has NO header metadata block content beyond version number in most cases.

Third-person character-study narrative. Carries MECHANISM_DEPTH / IDENTITY_STAGE / COST_TYPE / COST_INTENSITY / STORY_TYPE metadata block -- preserve byte-exact, translate body only. Named characters (Marcus, Elena, James, Priya, Devon, etc.) recur across personas -- see protected_terms.yaml for transliteration lock.

Sensory scene-setting prose, longer sentences than STORY. Imagery-dense -- preserve concrete sensory nouns (kitchen table, parking garage, elevator) exactly; these ground the metaphor and should not be abstracted in translation.
Condensed restatement of the chapter thesis. Short (avg 23 words). Preserve density -- do not pad with connective tissue in translation just to hit a word floor.

Core reframe line -- critical_recurring tier. Frequently follows a fixed skeleton (see duplicate_clusters.json 'pivot_threat_assessment', 'pivot_managing_task_message') with a topic-specific metaphor slotted in. Translate the CANONICAL metaphor consistently every time it recurs (glossary_project.yaml), not ad hoc per file.

Chapter-closing thesis. Short, aphoristic, high memorability requirement -- these are the lines most likely to be quoted/excerpted. Register and rhythm matter more than literal word-for-word fidelity here; still must pass semantic fidelity gate.

Second-person permission-giving language ('You are allowed to...', 'You don't have to...'). Reader-address register is the single highest-stakes voice element in this slot -- see style_guide_zh_tw.yaml reader_address.
Narrative throughline connecting chapter beats. Continuity-sensitive -- pronoun/name consistency with adjacent STORY cells must be verified in the book-level consistency pass.

Imperative, step-ordered practical instructions. Preserve step order and every explicit permission ('You are allowed to do nothing tonight'). Clinical/safety meaning must not be softened per translation-qa/SKILL.md rule 8.
Synthesis prose connecting mechanism to practice. One extreme outlier at 1,550 words -- verify per-file before batch translation, do not assume uniform length.

Named teacher's canonical doctrine text (non-production scope, deferred per zh_tw_translation_gap_audit_2026-07-15 SS7). Longest average word count of any slot. Voice is the named teacher persona's, not the generic narrator -- do not flatten to book-narrator voice.

Files under atoms/<persona>/anchored/<topic>/<slot>/CANONICAL.txt use a DIFFERENT header style ('--- variant: v01' not '## SLOT v01'). These are flagship/canonical narrative cells (critical_recurring tier) -- verify the translation-qa validator recognizes both header styles before batch dispatch; do not assume one parser handles both without checking.
